prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a c++ repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Open/Closed Principle (OCP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad APIs that sho...
raw_response:
```json
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "Catch2/extras/catch_amalgamated.hpp",
      "entity_name": "IEventListener",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 137,
      "line_end": 222,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Fat interface: IEventListener forces concrete reporters to implement many unrelated methods.",
      "reasoning": "The IEventListener interface defines a large number of virtual methods covering various test events (run, test case, section, assertion, benchmark, listing, etc.). Concrete reporters like AutomakeReporter or CompactReporter only care about a small subset of these events, leading to numerous empty or pass-through overrides in intermediate base classes (StreamingReporterBase, CumulativeReporterBase) or directly in concrete classes. This violates the Interface Segregation Principle (ISP), which is a facet of OCP, as clients are forced to depend on methods they don't use. Adding new event types would necessitate modifying all existing reporter classes, even if they don't handle the new event, to provide an empty implementation."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "Catch2/extras/catch_amalgamated.cpp",
      "entity_name": "Win32ColourImpl.use",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 3591,
      "line_end": 3616,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Switch statement on Colour::Code enum violates OCP.",
      "reasoning": "The 'use' method in Win32ColourImpl contains a switch statement that dispatches logic based on the 'Colour::Code' enum. This design violates OCP because adding a new color code would require modifying and recompiling this existing method, rather than extending the system with new behavior without altering existing code."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "Catch2/extras/catch_amalgamated.cpp",

This directly violates OCP because introducing a new color code would necessitate modifying and recompiling this existing method, rather than extending the system with new behavior without altering existing code."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "Catch2/extras/catch_amalgamated.cpp",
      "entity_name": "AssertionPrinter.print",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 9045,
      "line_end": 9179,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Switch statement on ResultWas::OfType enum violates OCP.",
      "reasoning": "The 'print' method in AssertionPrinter (and by extension, its derived classes) uses a series of 'case ResultWas::OfType' statements to determine how to format and print assertion results. This is a classic OCP violation. If a new 'ResultWas::OfType' is introduced, this method would need to be modified to handle the new type, requiring changes to existing, tested code."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "Catch2/extras/catch_amalgamated.cpp",

This is a classic OCP violation. If a new 'ResultWas::OfType' is introduced, this method would need to be modified to handle the new type, requiring changes to existing, tested code."
    }
  ]
}
```

findings.description: Fat interface: IEventListener forces concrete reporters to implement many unrelated methods.
findings.reasoning: The IEventListener interface defines a large number of virtual methods covering various test events (run, test case, section, assertion, benchmark, listing, etc.). Concrete reporters like AutomakeReporter or CompactReporter only care about a small subset of these events, leading to numerous empty or pass-through overrides in intermediate base classes (StreamingReporterBase, CumulativeReporterBase) or directly in concrete classes. This violates the Interface Segregation Principle (ISP), which is a facet of OCP, as clients are forced to depend on methods they don't use. Adding new event types would necessitate modifying all existing reporter classes, even if they don't handle the new event, to provide an empty implementation.

findings.description: Switch statement on Colour::Code enum violates OCP.
findings.reasoning: The 'use' method in Win32ColourImpl contains a switch statement that dispatches logic based on the 'Colour::Code' enum. This design violates OCP because adding a new color code would require modifying and recompiling this existing method, rather than extending the system with new behavior without altering existing code.

findings.description: Switch statement on Colour::Code enum violates OCP.
findings.reasoning: Similar to Win32ColourImpl, the 'use' method in ANSIColourImpl contains a switch statement that dispatches logic based on the 'Colour::Code' enum. This directly violates OCP because introducing a new color code would necessitate modifying and recompiling this existing method, rather than extending the system with new behavior without altering existing code.

findings.description: Switch statement on ResultWas::OfType enum violates OCP.
findings.reasoning: The 'print' method in AssertionPrinter (and by extension, its derived classes) uses a series of 'case ResultWas::OfType' statements to determine how to format and print assertion results. This is a classic OCP violation. If a new 'ResultWas::OfType' is introduced, this method would need to be modified to handle the new type, requiring changes to existing, tested code.
